era_context: The early Iron Age. Shalmaneser III's reign in Assyria. Greece in the Geometric period. Egypt fragmented. China's Western Zhou in decline. The Mediterranean is a zone of Phoenician commercial dominance and emerging Greek trade networks.

events.title: Shalmaneser III's military campaigns in Syria and the Levant
events.description: Shalmaneser III continues his systematic military campaigns in Syria and the Levant, consolidating Assyrian hegemony. These campaigns involve both direct military conquest and the establishment of tributary relationships with local rulers. The campaigns are documented in Assyrian royal inscriptions and represent the expansion of the Neo-Assyrian administrative and military system.
events.certainty_note: Shalmaneser III's campaigns are well-documented in royal inscriptions, but the precise dating of individual campaigns within his reign has some uncertainty. The inscriptions are organized by regnal year, but the absolute chronology is debated (±2–3 years).

events.title: Geometric pottery tradition in Greece
events.description: The Geometric period continues in Greece, with distinctive pottery production and trade. This period is characterized by abstract geometric designs and the gradual emergence of figurative representation. The pottery indicates growing inter-regional trade and cultural exchange among Greek communities.
events.certainty_note: The Geometric period is well-established through archaeological evidence. Dating within the period is approximate (±10–15 years).

disconfirming_evidence: No disconfirming evidence identified for events listed.
historiographic_note: Documentation for 778 BCE is sparse and regionally uneven. The Near East (Assyria) has the best documentation through royal inscriptions. Greece has archaeological evidence but no contemporary written records. Most of the world—Africa, northern Europe, Asia beyond the Near East, the Americas, and Oceania—has no written documentation for this year. The surviving sources are heavily biased toward imperial and elite narratives.

graph_edges.note: Shalmaneser III's campaigns in 778 BCE are part of his broader military program
